human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for fleet-mobility specificity, authority-source quality, lifecycle-state clarity, VIN and parts traceability, recall and unsafe-operation boundary handling, and validated spec-pack completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to fleet maintenance execution, work-order state handling, VIN-linked identity, parts traceability, blocked-work escalation, and completion evidence.

Authority sources cover FMCSA inspection and maintenance rules, NHTSA VIN and recall baselines, ISO asset-management control, GS1 traceability, Odette automotive component traceability, and CVSA out-of-service operating references.

Evaluation scenarios cover normal in-scope execution, missing prerequisites, unsupported completion, traceability conflicts, recall and out-of-service blocks, supplier substitution rejection, conflicting-policy escalation, execution exceptions, and tenant-specific adaptation.

The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/automotive-and-mobility/fleet-mobility-specialist/ so fleet mobility remains distinct from adjacent quality, production planning, inventory, procurement, supplier-quality, and logistics lanes.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.
Boundary enforcement remained strong around licensed inspection signoff, final safety release, engineering approval, supplier approval, warranty adjudication, and policy-override authority.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated fleet mobility specialist for work-order execution, downtime handling, VIN and parts traceability, recall and unsafe-operation escalation, and completion evidence capture.
Runtime remains specification-based until a separately promoted retained implementation exists.
